headline: Sabah Electricity Tariff 38% Higher Than Peninsula Rate
context: SESB charges residential consumers 38% more than TNB Peninsula rates averaging 45 sen per kWh versus 33 sen.

cards.big: Sabah residents pay 38% more for electricity than Peninsula households
cards.sub: Average tariff of 45 sen per kWh versus 33 sen reflects isolated grid costs

cards.big: SESB operates standalone grid serving 1.1 million connections with 55% diesel
cards.sub: Fuel cost dependency means Sabah tariffs fluctuate with global oil prices

cards.big: Federal government subsidises RM1.2 billion annually to keep SESB tariffs down
cards.sub: Without subsidy actual cost of Sabah electricity would exceed 62 sen per kWh

cards.big: Gas pipeline from Kimanis to Sandakan completed 2025 may reduce costs 15%
cards.sub: SESB projects tariff reduction only in 2028 after gas turbine conversion

cards.big: Electricity pricing tests whether essential service costs are equalized nationally
38% higher electricity tariff — Sabah subsidises peninsula rates through the very resources it produces.
